services.usageSource: gh repo view Aneety/ai --json visibility,isPrivate; gh run list --repo Aneety/ai --limit 200 --json createdAt,updatedAt,status,conclusion,event,workflowName

freeAllowance.label: standard GitHub-hosted runners in public repositories

services.calculation: Aneety/ai visibility is PUBLIC. 100.25 observed minutes in the latest 200 GitHub Actions runs for the June 2026 month-to-date window through 2026-06-29T00:33:53Z; divided by elapsed month ratio 0.932050540123 = 107.56 projected minutes. GitHub standard runners are free for public repositories, so projected cost is 0.

services.usageSource: Cloudflare API read from local .env: /accounts/:account_id/subscriptions and /accounts/:account_id/workers/scripts; sanitized output only. 2026-06-29 read returned Cloudflare Free Plan and 9 Workers after worker-relatorios publication.
services.calculation: Cloudflare API returned Cloudflare Free Plan and 9 current Workers after worker-relatorios publication. The new worker-pagamentos dashboard projects the account to 10 Workers, below the Workers Free allowance of 100 Workers per account.

services.usageSource: Versioned publication-evidence.json files for current Workers plus planned worker-pagamentos smokes: GET /health, GET /contract, SPA HTML/assets and POST /api/invoices/pdf, which server-side calls POST /reports/pdf.
services.calculation: Versioned smokes now include worker-relatorios /health, /contract and POST /reports/pdf plus existing Worker publication checks. The new worker-pagamentos smoke adds /health, /contract, SPA HTML/assets and POST /api/invoices/pdf. A conservative 1,000 requests/month projection stays below 100,000/day * 30 days = 3,000,000 requests/month on Workers Free.

services.service: Browser Run Quick Actions browser hours
services.usageSource: Cloudflare Browser Run is used only by worker-relatorios through binding Quick Actions. worker-pagamentos calls worker-relatorios server-side; smoke captures X-Browser-Ms-Used from the final PDF response.
services.calculation: Account is on Cloudflare Free Plan. worker-relatorios v1 uses Browser Run Quick Actions through env.BROWSER.quickAction("pdf", ...) instead of REST API or long-lived browser sessions. worker-pagamentos does not create another Browser Run binding; it calls worker-relatorios server-side with an operational token. The smoke captures X-Browser-Ms-Used, endpoint abuse risk is accepted for v1 without login, and the operational projection remains capped at 5 minutes/day, below the Browser Run Free allowance of 10 minutes/day.

services.usageSource: Static SQL inventory: find aneety-platform/apps -path */db-*/*.sql -type f; 21 SELECT statements across current D1 banco modules; no new D1 use in worker-relatorios or worker-pagamentos v1.
services.calculation: Conservative projection for one daily full D1 gate pass across current banco modules: 21 SELECT statements over tiny seed/fixture data rounded up to 120 rows read/day, below the Workers Free D1 allowance of 5,000,000 rows read/day. worker-relatorios v1 adds no D1 usage.

services.usageSource: Static SQL inventory: find aneety-platform/apps -path */db-*/*.sql -type f; 39 INSERT, 12 UPDATE, 48 CREATE and 43 DROP statements across current D1 banco modules; no new D1 use in worker-relatorios or worker-pagamentos v1.
services.calculation: Conservative projection for one daily full D1 gate pass across current banco modules: 39 INSERT + 12 UPDATE + 48 CREATE + 43 DROP statements = 142 potential written/DDL operations/day, below the Workers Free D1 allowance of 100,000 rows written/day. worker-relatorios v1 adds no D1 usage.

services.usageSource: D1 gate design creates one ephemeral database per validation and deletes it in cleanup; current SQL fixtures are tiny demo records and worker-relatorios/worker-pagamentos v1 add no storage.
services.calculation: Conservative storage projection for the remote D1 validation gate is 0.001 GB/account for ephemeral validation databases and tiny seed/fixture rows, below the Workers Free D1 total storage allowance of 5 GB. worker-relatorios v1 adds no R2, KV, Queue, D1 or persistent PDF storage.
